content.text:
{
  "dimension": "intervention-quality",
  "score": 3,
  "rationale": "The coach consistently refuses to match the client's list-recitation pace and refuses to strategize about Thursday, which is exactly what this scenario calls for. Move selection tracks the read in each turn: at T2 the coach catches the truncated sentence and invites it to complete; at T4 names the deflection pattern and pivots to feeling; at T6 deploys the false-end question ('what are you scared is going to happen if Thursday goes badly') which is the named breakthrough move for this scenario; at T8 explicitly names the bypass ('called fear motivation') and refuses the request to strategize ('I'm not going to do that yet') before going deeper with 'what would it mean about you'; at T10 drops to somatic; at T12 reflects the client's own words back as a question rather than arguing; at T14 holds the moment the client actually hears themselves. Sequence integrity is strong: feeling before action, want/fear under should, and the coach earns each next step. When somatic dropping at T10 didn't fully land (client deflected to 'just tense from desk'), the coach pivoted to a different angle (the 'always settle down' belief) rather than repeating. No breath practice was forced, no validation of overwhelm as situational, no 'you create your overwhelm.' The moves are AoA-specific and arriving at moments the client can just barely receive them.",
  "evidence_turns": [2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12, 14],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Catches the unfinished sentence rather than answering the prioritization question; refuses to match the rushing energy."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Names the deflection pattern ('that's not the point') and pivots from list to feeling — correct sequence move."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Deploys the false-end question almost exactly as the scenario's breakthrough move specifies. Excellent move-to-read match."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Names the reframe ('called fear motivation'), explicitly declines to strategize ('I'm not going to do that yet'), and goes under the fear with 'what would it mean about you' — earned next step."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Drops to somatic at the right moment, after the meaning-making has surfaced. Holds the client's own words back to them."},
    {"turn": 12, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "When the somatic move was partially deflected, coach pivots to questioning the 'always settle down' belief rather than re-pushing the body question."},
    {"turn": 14, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Notices the client hearing themselves and invites staying with it — perfectly timed micro-move that lets the realization land rather than rushing to interpret."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["the-rush-signature", "meet-resistance-with-okay", "what-are-you-defending", "overwhelm", "dont-add-the-overwhelm-of-creating-the-overwhelm", "dont-use-breath-to-repress"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["calibrate-the-threshold", "feeling-the-unfelt-emotion"],
  "notes": "The feeling has not yet been fully felt by turn 15 — the conversation ends just at the edge of the false end opening. The next move would need to calibrate the threshold ('what's the smallest amount you could let yourself feel where that goes') to fully complete the path, but everything up to this cutoff is well-timed and well-chosen."
}
